title: AI Full-stack Developer
description: Builds user-facing products that combine modern web interfaces, backend APIs, and responsibly integrated AI capabilities.
target_users: Students who enjoy building complete web applications
Early-career developers interested in AI-enabled products
sample_projects: Build a career guidance dashboard backed by a FastAPI service
Create an AI study planner with structured tool calls

market_relevance.reason: Organizations increasingly need engineers who can turn AI capabilities into usable, maintainable products.

explanation: This path can suit learners who enjoy connecting frontend experiences, backend services, and practical AI features.
safety_note: This recommendation is educational guidance only and does not guarantee employment outcomes.

title: AI Agent Engineer
description: Designs agent workflows that coordinate models, tools, contextual resources, safeguards, and evaluation.
target_users: Python learners interested in autonomous workflows
Developers who enjoy orchestration and system behavior
sample_projects: Build a guarded research agent that uses approved local tools
Create a multi-agent learning assistant with offline evaluations

market_relevance.reason: Agent-based systems are expanding, while reliable orchestration and evaluation remain developing engineering practices.

explanation: This path can fit learners who prefer designing reliable workflows and testing model behavior rather than only building prompts.
safety_note: This recommendation is educational guidance only and does not guarantee employment outcomes.

title: Backend AI Engineer
description: Builds reliable backend services that expose model capabilities through validated APIs and asynchronous workflows.
target_users: Backend developers interested in production AI integration
sample_projects: Create an API that queues and tracks AI document analysis jobs
Build a rate-aware model gateway with safe error responses

market_relevance.reason: AI-enabled products depend on dependable services, data access, observability, and API contracts.

explanation: This path may suit learners who value service reliability, data flows, and clean interfaces more than visual UI work.
safety_note: This recommendation is educational guidance only and does not guarantee employment outcomes.

title: Data Analyst
description: Cleans, explores, and communicates data to help teams answer practical business and product questions.
target_users: Students who enjoy finding patterns in structured data
Early-career learners interested in dashboards and decision support
sample_projects: Analyze learner engagement and present a reproducible dashboard
Clean a public dataset and explain three decision-relevant findings

market_relevance.reason: Teams across many sectors need people who can transform operational data into understandable evidence.

explanation: This path can fit learners who combine curiosity about data with clear communication and practical decision making.
safety_note: This recommendation is educational guidance only and does not guarantee employment outcomes.

title: Machine Learning Engineer
description: Develops, evaluates, and deploys machine learning systems with attention to reproducibility and operational quality.
target_users: Learners comfortable with Python and quantitative reasoning
Developers interested in model lifecycle engineering
sample_projects: Train and evaluate a transparent classifier on a public dataset
Package a small prediction service with monitoring notes

market_relevance.reason: Organizations need engineers who can move models from experiments into testable and maintainable services.

explanation: This path may fit learners who enjoy both model experimentation and the engineering needed to operate models responsibly.
safety_note: This recommendation is educational guidance only and does not guarantee employment outcomes.

title: Frontend Developer
description: Creates accessible, responsive interfaces that help users understand and interact with digital products.
target_users: Students who enjoy visual problem solving
Learners interested in browser-based product experiences
sample_projects: Build an accessible career exploration dashboard
Create a responsive learning tracker connected to a mock API

market_relevance.reason: Digital services continue to require usable, accessible interfaces across devices and user contexts.

explanation: This path can suit learners who care about interaction details, visual feedback, accessibility, and direct user impact.
safety_note: This recommendation is educational guidance only and does not guarantee employment outcomes.

title: DevOps / Cloud Engineer
description: Automates delivery, operates cloud services, and improves the reliability and visibility of software systems.
target_users: Learners who enjoy automation and infrastructure
Developers interested in reliable software delivery
sample_projects: Containerize and deploy a FastAPI service with health checks
Create a CI pipeline that validates and tests a student project

market_relevance.reason: Reliable delivery and cloud operations are foundational to maintaining modern software services.

explanation: This path may fit systematic learners who like automation, operational feedback, and improving how teams ship software.
safety_note: This recommendation is educational guidance only and does not guarantee employment outcomes.
